strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with well-defined contexts (photograph of John Bramblitt, personal bicycle, personal piano).
Explicit and consistent reference to the chosen IA prompt for each object, explaining how personal experience underpins knowledge gained.
Coherent justification for each objectâs inclusion, with most points supported by relevant detail or references to the prompt.
Effective structural organization, including a dedicated knowledge arguments section to overview key ideas.
weaknesses: Analysis relies heavily on anecdote and lacks deeper critical evaluation of key TOK concepts (e.g., ways of knowing, types of knowledge).
Repetitive justifications and occasional imprecision undermine the depth of the exhibitionâs argument.
Limited explicit engagement with specific ways of knowing (e.g., memory, sense perception) and areas of knowledge frameworks.
Several formatting and clarity issues (awkward sentences, broken citation, unexplained symbols) detract from overall coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs work meets the descriptors for the 7â8 band: all objects are specific and contextualized, links to the IA prompt are explicit and largely well explained, and each objectâs contribution is justified. However, the analysis remains conceptually thin in placesârelying on anecdote rather than sustained TOK argumentationâand there are lapses in precision, depth (notably in linking to ways of knowing), and academic rigour. These limitations place the work at the lower end of the band, justifying a final score of 7.

annotations.comment: The discussion lacks explicit reference to memory as a way of knowing; consider exploring how procedural memory contributes to riding a bicycle.
Avoid broad statements about âjoy of learningâ without linking them to specific knowledge claims or counterclaims within a TOK framework.

The student successfully identifies a specific real-world object (the photograph of John Bramblitt) and situates it within an identifiable professional art context, demonstrating a clear link to the exhibitionâs TOK framework.

The introductory description of John Bramblittâs background is largely descriptive and lacks an explicit connection to the IA prompt; the student should integrate elements of the prompt more directly here.
Consider engaging more deeply with the sensory details of the photographâsuch as texture and formâto illustrate how tactile perception emerges as a way of knowing in this context.
Strengthen the justification by explaining why this specific photograph was chosen over other images to demonstrate TOK principles, rather than simply stating it as Object 1.
The sentence beginning âdoes and to paintâ¦â is unclear and grammatically awkward; revise for precision and clarity to strengthen the argumentâs coherence.
Explicitly identify specific ways of knowingâsuch as sense perception or imaginationâwhen discussing Bramblittâs tactile techniques to deepen the TOK analysis.

The comparative claim about sighted versus non-sighted understanding of colour is interesting but remains unsupported by theoretical frameworks or examples; integrate a knowledge theory to substantiate this.
In the conclusion, link your findings back to the IA prompt using TOK terminology (e.g., personal versus shared knowledge) to reinforce the exhibitionâs coherence.
The isolated math symbol $$\mathbf{r}$$ appears without explanation or relevance; remove it or clarify its connection to the exhibition.
The vivid description of learning to ride a bicycle effectively situates the experience in a personal context, clearly demonstrating how personal experience contributes to practical knowledge.
Integrate TOK concepts by referencing specific ways of knowingâsuch as sense perception and emotionâto ground the analysis of each fall and scrape in TOK terminology.
The assertion that firsthand experience is âmore effective than adviceâ is presented as self-evident; strengthen this claim by citing an epistemological perspective or scholarly source.
The student connects personal achievement with knowledge acquisition, demonstrating conceptual awareness of how hands-on experience can shape understanding.
Expand on how scientific knowledge of mechanics (e.g., balance and motion) informs the learning process, perhaps by referencing the Natural Sciences as an area of knowledge.
The student effectively outlines a clear learning trajectory with the piano, situating personal context and initial challenges to illustrate knowledge development.
The term âmuscle memoryâ is used as a description but lacks discussion of its role as a way of knowing; link muscle memory to the Memory WOK in your analysis.
Deepen your analysis by exploring how emotional motivation and long-term practice interact as ways of knowing in your piano learning experience.
Explicitly restate how piano practice illustrates the relationship between personal experience and knowledge, ensuring a clear link back to the IA prompt in this section.
The inclusion of a dedicated knowledge arguments section provides a helpful overview of key ideas and demonstrates structural organization.
The knowledge arguments are expressed as generic assertions; each point should be developed with reference to specific objects or examples from your exhibition.
Transform the bullet points into analytical claims supported by evidence drawn from your three objects, explicitly citing relevant TOK concepts.
Several points overlap in meaning (e.g., points 1 and 2); consolidate or differentiate these arguments to avoid redundancy.
The reference to Trump (2023) contains a broken URL and spacing error in âknowledgeâ; verify and correct the URL formatting.
Adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA 7th edition) across all references and include access dates for online sources.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
